Updated April 2026 · 199 passports

Strongest passports of 2026

Every world passport ranked by "easy access" — the number of countries you can enter visa-free or with visa on arrival. Data from official government sources, cross-referenced monthly. An open, verifiable alternative to the Henley Passport Index.

199passports ranked
168top-tier reach
99global average

Bottom 10 — passports with most travel friction

These passports give holders the fewest visa-free or visa-on-arrival options. If you hold one, pairing it with a US Green Card, UAE residence permit, or Schengen residence permit can significantly expand your reach.

Rank Passport Easy access
#94 Afghanistan 26
#93 Syria 28
#92 Iraq 31
#91 Pakistan 32
#90 Somalia 33
#89 Yemen 35
#88 Palestine 36
#88 Bangladesh 36
#87 Nepal 39
#86 Sudan 40

Methodology

We rank passports by easy access — the count of destinations where you can arrive without arranging a visa in advance. That includes:

  • Visa-free (VF): No visa needed, walk across the border or through arrivals.
  • Visa on arrival (VoA): Visa issued at the destination airport or land border for a fee, no embassy visit.

We deliberately exclude from "easy access":

  • e-Visa (eV): Requires an online application before travel — still easier than embassy visas, but not immediate.
  • Electronic Travel Authorization (ETA): Same reasoning as e-visas.

Raw data comes from the open-source Passport Index Dataset, which cross-references IATA, government, and embassy sources. We update our copy monthly. Rankings tie frequently; in this table we break ties alphabetically.

Frequently asked questions

Which passport is the strongest in 2026?
The United Arab Emirates, South Korea, Spain passports top the ranking with visa-free or visa-on-arrival access to 168+ destinations — see the full table above. Rankings tie frequently; we break ties alphabetically in this table.
How is this different from the Henley Passport Index?
The Henley Passport Index uses IATA (airline) data and assigns a score; we use the open-source Passport Index Dataset which cross-references government sources and distinguishes visa-free, visa-on-arrival, and e-visa separately. Results are similar but not identical — we err on the side of strict definitions (e-visa is not "visa-free" here).
What counts as "easy access"?
We count visa-free (VF) plus visa on arrival (VoA) as "easy access" because both let you arrive without arranging a visa from an embassy. We deliberately exclude e-visa (eV) and electronic travel authorization (ETA) because they require an online application ahead of travel — still easy, but not the same.
Can I expand my passport's reach without a second passport?
Yes. Residence permits from countries like the UAE, US (Green Card), Schengen area, UK, and Canada grant you visa-free or visa-on-arrival access to additional countries on top of what your passport provides. See our UAE residence + Indian passport and US Green Card guides for examples.
Why is my passport ranked lower than I expected?
Rankings change as countries add or drop visa exemptions. Our data is verified from government sources and we update monthly. If you think a specific visa exemption is missing, please check the data against the source linked on the relevant passport page — and let us know if our data is out of date.
